name: Architects Engineers 9/11 Truth
description: The Architects & Engineers for 9/11 Truth (AE911Truth) is an organization promoting the conspiracy theory that the collapse of the World Trade Center towers and 7 World Trade Center on September 11, 2001, were the result of controlled demolitions rather than structural failure due to impact damage and subsequent fires. Proponents, primarily from architectural and engineering backgrounds, argue that the speed, completeness, and symmetry of the collapses, along with alleged physical evidence like molten metal and traces of explosives, contradict the official narrative presented by the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). They call for a new investigation into the events. The mainstream scientific and engineering consensus, based on extensive NIST investigations and peer-reviewed studies, firmly rejects these claims. NIST concluded that the collapses were initiated by structural damage caused by the impact of the airplanes and were accelerated by uncontrolled fires fueled by office materials, leading to a progressive failure of the buildings' support structures. Critics argue that AE911Truth misinterprets scientific data, ignores key findings from the NIST report, and promotes unsubstantiated claims based on selective evidence and flawed analysis. The theory originated in the years following the 9/11 attacks, gaining traction within the broader 9/11 conspiracy movement.
subtitle: Controlled demolition theory of 9/11 collapses.

claims.claim: The WTC buildings collapsed due to controlled demolition.
claims.evidence: Rapid, symmetrical collapse; molten metal found in debris; presence of thermite/thermate residue.
claims.criticism: NIST concluded collapses were due to fire-induced structural failure; 'molten metal' likely aluminum alloys from aircraft; thermite evidence disputed and attributed to other sources like primer paint.

claims.claim: WTC Building 7's collapse was not fire-induced.
claims.evidence: Freefall speed of collapse; symmetrical and rapid descent resembles controlled demolition.
claims.criticism: NIST explained the collapse through fire-induced weakening of a critical support column, leading to progressive failure; freefall speed was only for a brief period and not truly freefall.

claims.claim: The official NIST report is flawed and incomplete.
claims.evidence: Alleged inconsistencies in the report; lack of investigation into controlled demolition evidence.
claims.criticism: NIST conducted extensive investigations and simulations; its report is considered comprehensive by mainstream engineers; alternative explanations have been thoroughly debunked.
